evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a VLAN management system. I need a VLAN node
with an id attribute (just the VLAN number), a name, a status that should be a
dropdown (active/reserved/deprecated), and a role field. VLANs belong to a VLANGroup
which just has a name. Use namespace 'Ipam'.
evals.expected_output: A schema with correct attribute types (Dropdown not Text for status, Number for VLAN ID), attribute names >= 3 chars (not 'id'), proper kind references, and order_weight conventions.
expectations: The VLAN id attribute is renamed to meet the 3-char minimum (e.g., 'vlan_id' or 'vid'), since 'id' is only 2 characters
Status is kind: Dropdown with choice objects, NOT kind: Text
Attribute kind uses 'Text' not the deprecated 'String'
VLANGroup peer reference uses full kind 'IpamVlanGroup' (not just 'VlanGroup')
human_friendly_id is defined on both nodes
display_label is a singular Jinja2 string, NOT display_labels as a plural list
Schema starts with version: "1.0"

assertions.name: attr-min-length
assertions.check: VLAN id attribute renamed to >= 3 chars (e.g., vlan_id)

assertions.name: dropdown-for-status
assertions.check: Status uses kind: Dropdown with choice objects, not kind: Text

assertions.name: no-deprecated-string
assertions.check: Attribute kinds use 'Text' not the deprecated 'String'

assertions.name: full-kind-references
assertions.check: VLANGroup peer reference uses full kind 'IpamVlanGroup'

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id is defined on both VLAN and VLANGroup nodes

assertions.name: display-label-singular
assertions.check: Uses display_label (singular Jinja2 string), not deprecated display_labels

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a circuit management system. A Circuit connects
two endpoints (side A and side Z). Each CircuitEndpoint is installed in a specific
Device. A Provider offers multiple Circuits. Use namespace 'Circuit'. Don't forget
to make relationships properly bidirectional with matching identifiers on both sides.

Note: CircuitEndpoints are peers of the Circuit (not children), and Providers are
independent entities — use Attribute-kind relationships, not Component/Parent.
evals.expected_output: A schema with multiple bidirectional relationship pairs, all with matching identifiers. The two circuit endpoints should use Attribute kind (not Component/Parent since endpoints are peers of the circuit, not children).
expectations: Circuit-to-Provider relationship uses kind: Attribute (not Component/Parent) with matching identifiers on both sides
CircuitEndpoint-to-Device relationship uses kind: Attribute with matching identifiers on both sides
Circuit has two endpoint relationships (side_a and side_z or similar) each pointing to CircuitCircuitEndpoint
All relationship identifiers match between the two sides of each pair — no mismatched identifiers
All peer references use full namespace+name kind (e.g., CircuitProvider, CircuitCircuitEndpoint, not just 'Provider')
human_friendly_id is defined on Circuit and Provider nodes

assertions.name: attribute-kind-relationships
assertions.check: Circuit-to-Provider uses kind: Attribute with matching identifiers

assertions.name: endpoint-device-relationship
assertions.check: CircuitEndpoint-to-Device uses kind: Attribute with matching identifiers

assertions.name: two-endpoint-relationships
assertions.check: Circuit has two endpoint relationships (side_a/side_z) pointing to CircuitCircuitEndpoint

assertions.name: matching-identifiers
assertions.check: All relationship identifiers match between both sides of each pair

assertions.name: full-kind-references
assertions.check: All peer references use full namespace+name kind

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id on Circuit and Provider nodes

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a location hierarchy: Region > Site > Room > Rack.
Each level has a name and shortname. Objects should be easily identifiable in the UI.
Use namespace 'Location'.
evals.expected_output: A hierarchical schema using a generic with hierarchical: true, proper parent/children chain, human_friendly_id, and display_label (not deprecated display_labels).
expectations: A generic is defined with hierarchical: true
All location nodes inherit_from the hierarchical generic using its full kind
Root node (Region) has parent set to empty string or null
human_friendly_id is set on each node
display_label is used as a singular Jinja2 string, NOT the deprecated display_labels (plural list)
Schema starts with version: "1.0"
Parent/children chain is correct: Region->Site->Room->Rack

assertions.name: hierarchical-generic
assertions.check: A generic is defined with hierarchical: true

assertions.name: inherit-from-generic
assertions.check: All location nodes inherit_from the hierarchical generic using its full kind

assertions.name: root-no-parent
assertions.check: Root node (Region) has parent set to empty string or null

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id is set on each node

assertions.name: display-label-singular
assertions.check: Uses display_label (singular Jinja2 string), not deprecated display_labels

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

assertions.name: correct-hierarchy-chain
assertions.check: Parent/children chain: Region->Site->Room->Rack

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a rack-and-device inventory. A Rack contains many
Devices, and each Device must belong to exactly one Rack — a device cannot exist
without its rack. Each Rack belongs to exactly one Site. Use namespace 'Dcim'.

The Component (parent owns children) and Parent (child points to parent) relationships
must satisfy the server-side validation: cardinality, optional, and the rule that a
node cannot have more than one Parent relationship.
evals.expected_output: A schema with Component/Parent relationship pairs where the Parent side has cardinality: one and optional: false explicitly set, no node has more than one kind: Parent relationship, and identifiers match between both sides of each pair.
expectations: Each kind: Parent relationship has both cardinality: one AND optional: false explicitly set
No node declares more than one relationship with kind: Parent (server validates this)
Component/Parent pairs share matching identifiers on both sides
All peer references use the full kind (DcimRack, DcimSite, not just Rack/Site)
human_friendly_id is defined on each node
Schema starts with version: "1.0"

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

assertions.name: parent-rel-optional-false
assertions.check: Every kind: Parent relationship has optional: false and cardinality: one

assertions.name: parent-rel-single
assertions.check: No node has more than one kind: Parent relationship

assertions.name: matching-identifiers
assertions.check: Component/Parent identifier pairs match on both sides

assertions.name: full-kind-references
assertions.check: All peer references use full Namespace+Name kind

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id defined on each node

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a BGP autonomous-system catalog. An AutonomousSystem
node has an asn (number) and a description. The display name should NOT be entered by
the user — it must be auto-derived from the ASN as 'AS<asn>' (e.g., 'AS65000') using a
Jinja2 computed attribute. The derived name is used as the display label and as part
of human_friendly_id, so it must always be populated. Use namespace 'Routing'.
evals.expected_output: A schema where the name attribute uses computed_attribute with kind: Jinja2 and a non-empty jinja2_template, paired with read_only: true (always required) and optional: false (because the value is load-bearing — used as display_label and in human_friendly_id).
expectations: The name attribute uses computed_attribute with kind: Jinja2 and a jinja2_template that derives from asn__value
read_only: true is set (mandatory whenever computed_attribute is present — Infrahub validates this)
optional: false because the value is load-bearing (display label + human_friendly_id)
Attribute kinds use 'Text' not the deprecated 'String'
All peer references (if any) use the full namespace+name kind
Schema starts with version: "1.0"

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

assertions.name: computed-jinja2-readonly
assertions.check: Every computed_attribute pairs with read_only: true

assertions.name: computed-jinja2-kind
assertions.check: computed_attribute uses kind: Jinja2 with a jinja2_template

assertions.name: no-deprecated-string
assertions.check: Attribute kinds use 'Text' not deprecated 'String'

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a load-balancer VIP service. A VIPService has a
name (Text) and references a frontend IP (IpamIPAddress). The VIPService owns a list
of HealthCheck objects — health checks have no meaning on their own and should be
deleted when their VIPService is deleted. The frontend IP, however, is shared
infrastructure: deleting a VIPService must NOT delete its IP. Use namespace 'ServiceLB'.
evals.expected_output: A schema where the VIPService → HealthCheck Component relationship sets on_delete: cascade explicitly (so health checks are removed when the parent VIP is deleted), and the VIPService → IP relationship either omits on_delete or sets on_delete: no-action (so the shared IP is preserved). Any kind: Parent relationship has optional: false.
expectations: At least one relationship sets on_delete: cascade — the owned HealthCheck children whose existence has no meaning without the VIPService
The IP-address reference does NOT set on_delete: cascade (no-action or omitted, since the IP is shared infrastructure)
Any kind: Parent relationship has optional: false and cardinality: one
Component/Parent identifier pairs match on both sides
All peer references use the full namespace+name kind (e.g., IpamIPAddress, not IPAddress)
human_friendly_id is defined on user-facing nodes
Schema starts with version: "1.0"

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

assertions.name: on-delete-cascade-present
assertions.check: At least one relationship sets on_delete: cascade for owned children

assertions.name: parent-rel-optional-false
assertions.check: Any kind: Parent relationship has optional: false and cardinality: one

assertions.name: matching-identifiers
assertions.check: Component/Parent identifier pairs match on both sides

assertions.name: full-kind-references
assertions.check: All peer references use full Namespace+Name kind

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id defined on user-facing nodes

evals.prompt:
Create an Infrahub schema for a network-device inventory. Define a generic
GenericDevice (with shared attributes like hostname and role) and a concrete Device
node that inherits from it. The concrete Device should:

  - Be eligible for rendered configuration artifacts (so artifact definitions in
    .infrahub.yml can attach to it).
  - Be cloneable from the UI as a starter for new devices (one template per role —
    leaf, spine, edge — speeds onboarding).

The two capabilities are independent features and should be applied to the concrete
Device, not to the generic. Use namespace 'Dcim'.
evals.expected_output: A schema with the GenericDevice generic carrying the shared attributes, and a concrete Device node that both inherits from CoreArtifactTarget AND sets generate_template: true. Neither flag appears on the generic.
expectations: The concrete Device node inherits from CoreArtifactTarget so artifacts can attach to instances
The concrete Device node sets generate_template: true so users can clone it as a starter
Neither CoreArtifactTarget inheritance nor generate_template appears on the GenericDevice generic (generics cannot be artifact targets and are not instantiable as templates)
The Device inherits from the GenericDevice generic using its full kind (DcimGenericDevice)
human_friendly_id is defined on the concrete Device node
Schema starts with version: "1.0"

assertions.name: schema-version
assertions.check: Schema starts with version: '1.0'

assertions.name: core-artifact-target-concrete
assertions.check: CoreArtifactTarget is inherited by the concrete Device, never by the generic

assertions.name: generate-template-concrete-only
assertions.check: generate_template: true appears on the concrete Device, never on the generic

assertions.name: human-friendly-id
assertions.check: human_friendly_id defined on the concrete Device
